title: circt-opt cannot consume the generic format it generates
body:
`circt-opt` cannot take as input the output it emitted using `-mlir-print-op-generic`. 

The reason this happens seems to be because of the location attributes attached to HwModule. Are they truly necessary? It seems to fail because they are inconsistent with block arguments. But is this check necessary? And if the locations can be found from the block arguments, are the explicit locations necessary?

## Instructions to reproduce

Give the following snippet to `circt-opt -mlir-print-op-generic`:

```mlir
hw.module @B(%a: i1) -> (nameOfPortInSV: i1, "": i1) {
  %0 = comb.or %a, %a : i1
  %1 = comb.and %a, %a : i1
  hw.output %0, %1: i1, i1
}
```

This should yield something like:
```
#loc = loc("<stdin>":1:14)
#loc1 = loc("<stdin>":1:26)
#loc2 = loc("<stdin>":1:46)
"builtin.module"() ({
  "hw.module"() ({
  ^bb0(%arg0: i1):
    %0 = "comb.or"(%arg0, %arg0) : (i1, i1) -> i1
    %1 = "comb.and"(%arg0, %arg0) : (i1, i1) -> i1
    "hw.output"(%0, %1) : (i1, i1) -> ()
  }) {argLocs = [#loc], argNames = ["a"], comment = "", function_type = (i1) -> (i1, i1), parameters = [], resultLocs = [#loc1, #loc2], resultNames = ["nameOfPortInSV", ""], sym_name = "B"} : () -> ()
}) : () -> ()
```

Giving this output to `circt-opt` again yields an error:
```
<stdin>:5:3: error: 'hw.module' op block argument locations should match signature locations
  "hw.module"() ({
  ^
```
